full_prompt: Design an automotive engine mount bracket for a 4-cylinder 2.0 L passenger-vehicle engine in the spirit of a canonical automotive-engine-mount patent (US10189326B2-style), with dual targets: (a) vibration isolation tuned to NVH-favorable frequencies and (b) crash-load structural integrity per FMVSS 301. Envelope: 200 x 150 x 100 mm. Interfaces: engine-block side has two M10 x 1.25 bolts on 70 mm spacing at the top; body-side (subframe/rail) has three M12 x 1.25 bolts in a triangle 120 x 60 mm. The bracket carries a cast-in elastomer isolator (NR rubber, durometer 60 Shore A) as an over-molded center element, with the bracket being the metallic load path. Metal: AlSi10Mg die-cast or cast aluminum AA319-T6 (yield 165 MPa, UTS 230 MPa, E 74 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Engine mass supported by three mounts; this one carries 1/3 of a 160 kg powertrain = 53 kg + fuel-tank harmonic resonance consideration. Load cases: LC1 static engine weight 1/3 x 160 kg x 9.81 = 523 N downward at isolator; LC2 engine torque reaction at 2x max torque 400 N*m -> at 100 mm arm gives 4 kN transverse; LC3 automotive NVH design target first-mode natural frequency of the bracket metal-only structure >= 350 Hz (classical rule: engine idle rotational frequency ~30 Hz, combustion 2nd order ~60 Hz, driver-audible NVH peak avoid 250-300 Hz, so first bracket mode >=350 Hz to avoid audible excitation); LC4 FMVSS 301 rear-impact 50 km/h crash deceleration 40 g applied to engine mass = 53 * 40 * 9.81 = 20.8 kN; LC5 FMVSS 214 side impact 25 g = 13 kN lateral. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises at LC1+LC2 static-operational combined <= yield / 2.0 = 82.5 MPa (automotive chassis SF 2.0 on yield, ISO 16750-3 analog); (R2) max von Mises at LC4 crash <= UTS / 1.2 = 192 MPa (crash SF 1.2 on UTS per FMVSS 301 energy-management practice); (R3) first natural frequency of metal bracket >= 350 Hz; (R4) elastomer isolator Shore A 60 stiffness chosen such that mount system natural frequency in the vertical direction is 10-15 Hz (away from idle 30 Hz + 1st-order 17 Hz and body natural 5-7 Hz); (R5) mass <= 1.2 kg; (R6) fatigue life 10^7 cycles at LC2 amplitude using AlSi10Mg cast S-N (fatigue limit ~80 MPa for AA319-T6 at 10^7). Deliverables: STEP AP242 + over-mold elastomer geometry; FEA (static + modal + crash) + NVH frequency report.
short_prompt:
Design an automotive engine mount bracket for a 4-cylinder 2.0 L passenger-vehicle engine in the spirit of a canonical automotive-engine-mount patent (US10189326B2-style), with dual targets: NVH-tuned vibration isolation and FMVSS 301 crash-load structural integrity.

Envelope: 200 x 150 x 100 mm. Engine-block side: two M10 x 1.25 bolts on 70 mm spacing. Body-side: three M12 x 1.25 bolts in a triangle 120 x 60 mm. An over-molded cast-in elastomer isolator (NR rubber, Shore A 60) sits at the center element; the bracket is the metallic load path.

Material: AlSi10Mg die-cast or AA319-T6.

Engine mass: 1/3 of 160 kg powertrain = 53 kg on this mount.

Load cases.
- LC1 static engine weight: 1/3 x 160 kg x 9.81 = 523 N at isolator.
- LC2 engine torque reaction at 2x max torque 400 N*m -> 4 kN transverse at 100 mm arm.
- LC3 bracket metal-only first-mode natural frequency >= 350 Hz.
- LC4 FMVSS 301 rear-impact 50 km/h crash: 40 g x 53 kg = 20.8 kN.
- LC5 FMVSS 214 side impact: 25 g = 13 kN lateral.

Additional gates: mount-system vertical natural frequency 10-15 Hz (avoiding idle 30 Hz, 1st-order 17 Hz, body 5-7 Hz); mass <= 1.2 kg; fatigue life 10^7 cycles at LC2 amplitude (AA319-T6 S-N limit ~80 MPa at 10^7).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Elastomer stiffness + mass -> system frequency is a 1-DOF closed-form.
LC4/LC5 crash events are explicit transient with large deformation, outside classical linear FEA.

prompt.title: Cast aluminum engine mount bracket with elastomer isolator (automotive-engine-mount patent US10189326B2 style)
prompt.brief: Design a 200x150x100 mm AA319-T6 engine mount with NR-rubber isolator; tune 1st metal mode >=350 Hz and mount-system vertical mode 10-15 Hz; survive FMVSS 301 40 g crash.

boundary_conditions.engine_interface: engine mass lumped on isolator, elastomer stiffness modeled

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: away from idle 30 Hz + body natural 5-7 Hz

requirements.governing_standard: FMVSS 301 + FMVSS 214 + automotive NVH practice (public-domain framework) + SAE J1099

analysis_types: linear static + modal + crash transient + fatigue post-proc

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Engine bay ambient up to 100 C is within AA319 working range.

notes.patent_fetch: US10189326B2 specific values not confirmed. Natural frequency 350 Hz and 10-15 Hz system mode are industry-typical NVH targets.
notes.numeric_source: FMVSS 301/214 crash envelopes + SAE J1099 fatigue + AA319-T6 allowables.

eval_coverage: NVH first-mode tuning + system frequency range
